companies.name: Construction Robotics
companies.description: Developer of SAM100 (Semi-Automated Mason), a bricklaying robot that assists masons. Also makes MULE for lifting materials up to 135kg.

companies.name: Monumental
companies.description: Amsterdam-based startup developing autonomous bricklaying robots for construction sites.

companies.name: FBR (Fastbrick Robotics)
companies.description: Developer of Hadrian X, a fully automated bricklaying robot mounted on a truck. Builds walls from 3D models with high precision.

companies.name: Conjet
companies.description: Robotic hydrodemolition solutions for concrete removal and surface preparation using high-pressure water jets.

companies.name: Advanced Construction Robotics (ACR)
companies.description: Developer of TyBOT (autonomous rebar tying) and IronBOT (rebar placement). Self-navigates, self-ties on construction sites.
companies.funding: ~$30M Series A+B (Bessemer Venture Partners, Eclipse Ventures)

companies.name: Okibo
companies.description: AI-guided painting and drywall finishing robots. EG7 robot has covered 1M+ sq ft in commercial applications.

companies.name: Canvas
companies.description: World's first robotic drywall finishing company. Level 4 and Level 5 finishes. Partnered with Hilti for manufacturing.

companies.name: Path Robotics
companies.description: AI-based robotic welding systems for heavy construction equipment, trailers, utility structures, shipbuilding, bridge structures.

companies.name: Brokk
companies.description: World's leading manufacturer of remote-controlled demolition robots for construction, mining, and tunneling.

companies.name: nLink
companies.description: Mobile robots for drilling, surveying, and layout tasks on construction sites.

companies.name: ICON
companies.description: Leading 3D-printed construction company. Built communities with Lennar in Austin, TX. NASA habitat challenge finalist. Vulcan printer system.

companies.name: COBOD International
companies.description: Developer of BOD2 3D construction printer. Building first 3D-printed school in Ukraine. Used by Alquist in Virginia.

companies.name: Apis Cor
companies.description: Printed the world's largest 3D-printed building. NASA Habitat Challenge finalist. Mobile 3D printer system.

companies.name: Mighty Buildings
companies.description: 3D-printed homes using patented light stone material. Building communities. Energy-efficient, sustainable.
companies.key_people: Slava Solonitsyn (Co-Founder — note: Anton's former colleague from Glance/Mighty Buildings era)

companies.name: SQ4D
companies.description: 3D-printed homes using ARCS (Autonomous Robotic Construction System).

companies.name: CyBe Construction
companies.description: Concrete 3D printing technology for construction.

companies.name: XtreeE
companies.description: Large-scale 3D printing for construction. Concrete printing.

companies.name: Tvasta Manufacturing Solutions
companies.description: India's first 3D-printed house. Construction 3D printing technology.

companies.name: Aridditive
companies.description: Concrete 3D printing technology.

companies.name: Branch Technology
companies.description: Freeform 3D printing for construction. Large-scale additive manufacturing.

companies.name: CONCR3DE
companies.description: Binder jetting 3D printing for concrete and stone construction materials.

companies.name: Winsun / Gaudi Tech
companies.description: Pioneer in 3D-printed construction. Printed 10 houses in 24 hours. Now rebranded as Gaudi Tech.

companies.name: Dusty Robotics
companies.description: FieldPrinter robot for automated construction layout. Prints BIM designs directly on floors.

companies.name: Buildots
companies.description: AI-powered progress tracking using 360° cameras. Compares site reality to BIM models.

companies.name: Doxel
companies.description: AI-powered progress tracking using lidar scanners. Measures installed work and verifies quality.

companies.name: OpenSpace
companies.description: Automated 360° site capture and documentation platform. AI-powered comparison.

companies.name: Reconstruct
companies.description: Reality capture and digital twin platform for construction and facility management.

companies.name: HoloBuilder
companies.description: 360° photo documentation platform for construction progress tracking.

companies.name: Skycatch
companies.description: Drone-based surveying and data collection for construction sites. Autonomous flight and data processing.

companies.name: Built Robotics
companies.description: Exosystem autonomy kit for construction equipment (excavators, dozers, CTLs). Retrofits existing machines.

companies.name: SafeAI
companies.description: Autonomous retrofit technology for heavy equipment in mining and construction.

companies.name: Teleo
companies.description: Semi-autonomous retrofit for heavy equipment. Remote operation with autonomous capabilities.

companies.name: Pronto AI
companies.description: Autonomous trucking technology for construction and mining.

companies.name: DJI Enterprise
companies.description: World's largest drone manufacturer. Enterprise division focuses on construction surveying, inspection, and monitoring.

companies.name: DroneDeploy
companies.description: Drone mapping and inspection platform for construction, agriculture, and energy.

companies.name: Skydio
companies.description: AI-powered autonomous drones for inspection, mapping, and site monitoring.

companies.name: Percepto
companies.description: Autonomous drone-in-a-box for industrial inspection, including construction.

companies.name: American Robotics
companies.description: FAA-approved fully automated drone system for industrial sites.

companies.name: Ekso Bionics (EksoWorks)
companies.description: Industrial exoskeletons for construction workers performing lifting, drilling, and overhead work.

companies.name: Hilti
companies.description: Jaibot (semi-automated drilling), exo-01 exoskeleton, and manufacturer behind Canvas drywall robot. Major construction tools company.

companies.name: German Bionic
companies.description: AI-powered exoskeletons for industrial applications including construction.

companies.name: Sarcos Robotics
companies.description: Full-body industrial exoskeletons (Guardian XO) and snake robots for inspection.

companies.name: SuitX (acquired by Ottobock)
companies.description: Modular exoskeletons for industrial workers. Acquired by Ottobock.

companies.name: Hirebotics (Cobot Welder)
companies.description: Collaborative robot welding systems. Cobot welder for construction applications.

companies.name: Kewazo
companies.description: Robotic material transport for construction sites (scaffolding, construction materials). Liftbot system.

companies.name: Versatile
companies.description: IoT and AI platform for crane operations. Real-time monitoring, load optimization, safety.

companies.name: Q-Bot
companies.description: Robotic underfloor insulation and surveying for existing buildings.

companies.name: Veev
companies.description: Closed-wall prefab construction system. Integrated MEP systems in factory-built panels.

companies.name: Boxabl
companies.description: Foldable, factory-built housing units. Casita model. Mass production for affordable housing.

companies.name: Katerra (assets sold)
companies.description: Was a major construction technology company. Filed bankruptcy 2021. Assets sold. Notable as cautionary tale.

companies.name: Boston Dynamics
companies.description: Atlas humanoid robot and Spot quadruped. Used in construction for surveying, demolition assistance, BIM modeling.

companies.name: Fischer International (BauBot)
companies.description: BauBot — Swiss Army knife of construction robots. Lifting, drilling, cutting, dust extraction.

companies.name: Scaled Robotics
companies.description: Autonomous site scanning and progress monitoring robots.

companies.name: Hyperion Robotics
companies.description: 3D concrete printing for construction. Sustainable concrete solutions.

companies.name: 14Trees (Holcim)
companies.description: Joint venture with Holcim for 3D-printed housing in Africa. Using COBOD printers.

companies.name: Alquist 3D
companies.description: 3D-printed homes using COBOD printers. Building in Virginia with state grants.

companies.name: 博智林 (Botai / Bright Dream Robotics)
companies.description: Subsidiary of Country Garden. China's leading construction robotics company. 28+ commercial robots, 2000+ patents (1318 granted inventions), deployed across 31 provinces, 650+ projects, 2100+ units delivered, 2000万+ sqm construction. Products cover concrete finishing, bricklaying/plastering, interior decoration, exterior painting, basement renovation. Five research institutes. Led creation of China's first construction robotics standards body (CECS).

companies.name: 特辰科技 (Te Chen Technology)
companies.description: Specializes in construction protection robots and wall construction robots for high-altitude work. Also develops prefabricated building robots.

companies.name: 中建科技 (CSCEC Tech)
companies.description: Technology arm of China State Construction Engineering (CSCEC). Develops prefabricated building smart construction robots for light steel keel partitions, brick stacking, floor assembly.

companies.name: 蔚建科技 (WeBuild Technology)
companies.description: "Robot + Intelligent Construction" focused. Develops plastering robots, painting robots, and rebar processing. Won national "Little Giant" designation (专精特新). Won Shanghai Magnolia Award (highest construction quality honor). Core tech in precision mechanics, navigation, vision, AI.

companies.name: 大界机器人 (Dajie Robot / RoboticPlus.AI)
companies.description: Construction robotics company in Yangtze River Delta region. Part of China's construction robot industry ecosystem.

companies.name: 筑橙科技 (Zhucheng Technology)
companies.description: Recognized by MIIT (Ministry of Industry and Information Technology) for outstanding construction robot application scenarios.

companies.name: 丰坦机器人 (Fengtan Robot)
companies.description: Construction robot company. Listed among industry representative companies in 2025 reports.

companies.name: 中联重科 (Zoomlion)
companies.description: Major construction equipment manufacturer. Since 2024, ventured into embodied-intelligence humanoid robotics for construction. Building on fully integrated in-house development.

companies.name: 三一重工 (SANY)
companies.description: China's largest construction equipment manufacturer. Developing intelligent and autonomous construction equipment.

companies.name: Shimizu Corporation
companies.description: Major Japanese general contractor with decades of construction robotics R&D. Robots for excavating, material handling, concrete placing, finishing, fireproofing.

companies.name: Obayashi Corporation
companies.description: Leading Japanese GC. Active in autonomous hauling, construction automation, and smart construction.

companies.name: Takenaka Corporation
companies.description: One of Japan's oldest companies. Active construction robotics R&D since 1970s-80s.

companies.name: Kajima Corporation
companies.description: Major Japanese GC with construction robotics R&D programs.

companies.name: 大林組 (Obayashi — already listed) / 清水建設 (Shimizu — already listed) / Kajima Construction Robotics Division
HD Hyundai Robotics
companies.description: Hyundai Heavy Industries group. Developing robots for construction and industrial applications.

companies.name: The Boring Company
companies.description: Elon Musk's tunnel boring company. Autonomous tunnel construction.

companies.name: Printed Farms
companies.description: 3D-printed construction in Florida.

companies.name: Emergent 3D
companies.description: 3D-printed construction.

companies.name: Citizen Robotics
companies.description: Community-focused 3D-printed housing.

companies.name: AI Build
companies.description: Robotic 3D printing for large-scale construction applications.

companies.name: ACCIONA
companies.description: Major infrastructure company investing in 3D construction printing.

companies.name: Royal Truck & Equipment
companies.description: Autonomous truck-mounted attenuators for highway construction safety.

companies.name: Thinking Huts
companies.description: 3D-printed schools for developing regions.

companies.name: WASP
companies.description: Large-scale 3D printing for architecture and construction.

companies.name: Renovate Robotics
companies.description: Developer of Rufus, an automated roofing robot designed to double productivity for asphalt shingle installation and improve safety for roofing contractors. The robot uses a winch-based system and moves across the roof in a gantry-like X,Y axis pattern installing shingles.
companies.funding: Raised $2.5 million in a pre-seed round led by Alley Robotics Ventures

companies.name: Bedrock Robotics
companies.description: Startup founded by former Waymo engineers developing an autonomous, aftermarket platform for construction equipment. Plans to launch autonomous technology for excavators and other heavy machinery by 2026.
companies.key_people: Boris Sofman (CEO, former Waymo engineer), Ajay Gummalla, Kevin Peterson, Tom Eliaz

companies.name: AIM Intelligent Machines
companies.description: Develops autonomous technology for heavy equipment that runs continuously at peak performance. Uses a rigorous 3-step process to take customers from manual operation to autonomous operation. Commercially deployed at mine sites and construction sites worldwide.

companies.name: Gravis Robotics
companies.description: Develops autonomous earthmoving technology that retrofits construction machines with the Gravis RACK, sensors and intuitive touchscreen control to augment crews for safer sites and superhuman productivity. Increases throughput by up to 30% with terrain-aware excavation.
companies.funding: Seed funding from btov Partners and others

companies.name: Hilti Jaibot
companies.description: Semi-automated drilling robot for construction sites. Developed by Hilti as part of their construction robotics line, designed for overhead drilling and installation work.

companies.name: Sarcos Guardian XO
companies.description: Full-body powered industrial exoskeleton that enables users to lift heavy objects with ease, reducing strain and fatigue. Designed for industrial and construction applications.

companies.name: EksoVest
companies.description: Lightweight, passive exoskeleton vest designed to support workers during overhead tasks and reduce shoulder fatigue. Used in construction for drilling, fastening, and overhead work.

companies.name: German Bionic Apogee
companies.description: Latest generation AI-powered exoskeleton for industrial applications including construction. Features lightweight design and enhanced AI capabilities for motion prediction and assistance.

companies.name: Versatile AI
companies.description: IoT and AI platform for crane operations providing real-time monitoring, load optimization, and safety features for construction cranes.

companies.name: Q-Bot
companies.description: Develops robotic underfloor insulation and surveying solutions for existing buildings. Uses robots to apply insulation and survey building interiors.
